name: LangGraph
summary: Stateful graph-based agent orchestration from LangChain team. Cyclic graphs with persistence and human-in-the-loop. Tightly coupled to LangChain ecosystem but best-in-class for complex agent workflows.

name: Semantic Kernel
summary: Microsoft's LLM orchestration SDK. Strong Azure/OpenAI integration with planner-based agent patterns. Enterprise-oriented; Python SDK lags behind C# in features.

name: Spring AI
summary: Spring ecosystem's LLM framework for Java/Kotlin. Brings familiar Spring patterns (dependency injection, auto-config) to AI apps. Strong for Java shops, irrelevant for Python-first teams.

name: Mastra
summary: TypeScript-first AI agent framework with built-in tool calling, RAG, and workflow engine. Early but growing fast in the JS/TS ecosystem.

name: CrewAI
summary: Role-based multi-agent framework. Intuitive crew/agent/task abstraction for orchestrating multiple LLM agents. Popular for prototyping but can be opaque for complex production workflows.

name: AutoGen
summary: Microsoft's multi-agent conversation framework. v0.4 rewrite (AG2) is event-driven and modular. Strong research backing but API instability between versions has frustrated adopters.

name: Smolagents
summary: Hugging Face's minimalist agent library. Code-first agents that write Python to solve tasks. Lightweight alternative to heavier frameworks; good HF Hub integration.

name: Agency Swarm
summary: OpenAI Assistants API-based multi-agent framework. Focuses on inter-agent communication via shared threads. Tightly coupled to OpenAI; limited provider flexibility.

name: Phidata
summary: Agent framework with built-in memory, knowledge bases, and tool use. Clean Pydantic-based API. Rebranded to Agno in late 2025; growing community.

name: Claude Agent SDK
summary: Anthropic's official agent SDK for building Claude-powered agents. Tight integration with Claude models, tool use, and computer use. Single-provider lock-in but best-in-class for Claude workflows.

name: OpenAI Agents SDK
summary: OpenAI's official agent framework (successor to Swarm). Handoffs, guardrails, and tracing built-in. Simple API but OpenAI-only; designed for production agent deployments.

name: Google ADK
summary: Google's Agent Development Kit for Gemini-powered agents. Deep Vertex AI integration with session management and tool orchestration. Very new; Google ecosystem lock-in.

name: Agno
summary: Rebrand of Phidata. Multi-modal agent framework with memory, knowledge, and reasoning. Model-agnostic with clean API. Growing rapidly in the agent framework space.

name: DSPy
summary: Stanford's programming framework for LLMs — replaces hand-written prompts with optimizable modules. Compiler-based prompt optimization with metric-driven tuning. Paradigm shift but steep learning curve.

name: Instructor
summary: Pydantic-based structured output extraction from LLMs. Patches provider SDKs to return validated Pydantic models. Simple, elegant, and provider-agnostic. Best-in-class for structured extraction.

name: LMQL
summary: SQL-like query language for LLMs with constraints and control flow. Novel approach to structured prompting but adoption has stalled as native structured output from providers improved.

name: Guidance
summary: Microsoft-backed constrained generation library. Template-based prompting with guaranteed output structure via token-level control. Powerful for local models; less relevant for API-only usage.

name: Outlines
summary: Structured generation via grammar-constrained decoding. Guarantees valid JSON/regex/Pydantic output at inference time. Integrates with vLLM and HF. Best for local model structured output.

name: SGLang
summary: High-performance LLM serving with RadixAttention for prefix caching and structured generation. Competitive with vLLM on throughput. Strong HPC fit with multi-GPU support.

name: Marvin
summary: Prefect-backed library for AI functions, classification, extraction, and image generation. Decorator-based API that makes LLM calls feel like normal Python functions. Clean but narrow scope.

name: llama.cpp
summary: C/C++ LLM inference engine with broad hardware support. Foundation for GGUF quantized model ecosystem. Runs on CPU, GPU, and edge devices. De facto standard for local inference.

name: Jan
summary: Desktop app for running LLMs locally with ChatGPT-like UI. OpenAI-compatible API server built on llama.cpp. Good for personal use; not designed for production serving.

name: LM Studio
summary: Polished desktop app for local LLM inference. Model discovery, download, and OpenAI-compatible API. Free for personal use. Popular with non-technical users; limited programmatic control.

name: Llamafile
summary: Mozilla-backed single-file LLM executable. Bundles model weights + inference engine into one cross-platform binary using Cosmopolitan libc. Zero-install deployment; great for air-gapped environments.

name: ExLlamaV2
summary: High-performance GPTQ/EXL2 quantized inference for NVIDIA GPUs. Fastest single-GPU inference for quantized models. Narrow focus but excellent at what it does.

name: MLC LLM
summary: Machine Learning Compilation for LLMs. Compiler-based approach for deploying to diverse hardware (GPU, mobile, browser via WebGPU). From TVM team. Good for edge/mobile deployment.

name: TensorRT-LLM
summary: NVIDIA's optimized LLM inference library. Maximum throughput on NVIDIA GPUs with tensor parallelism and in-flight batching. Complex setup but unmatched performance on NVIDIA hardware.

name: FAISS
summary: Meta's GPU-accelerated similarity search library. Gold standard for raw vector search performance. Not a database (no persistence, CRUD); use as a building block. Excellent for HPC with GPU support.

name: Unstructured
summary: Document parsing and chunking for RAG pipelines. Handles PDFs, HTML, DOCX, images with OCR. Essential preprocessing step before embedding. Heavy system dependencies for full feature set.

name: RAGAS
summary: RAG evaluation framework with reference-free metrics (faithfulness, relevancy, context recall). LLM-as-judge approach. De facto standard for RAG evaluation. Requires LLM API calls for scoring.

name: DeepEval
summary: Pytest-integrated LLM evaluation framework. 14+ metrics including hallucination, bias, toxicity. Feels like writing unit tests for LLM outputs. Growing fast as LLM testing becomes standard.

name: Promptfoo
summary: CLI-first LLM evaluation and red-teaming tool. YAML config for test cases, side-by-side model comparison, and CI integration. Best for prompt regression testing and model selection.

name: Giskard
summary: AI model testing framework covering both traditional ML and LLMs. Automated vulnerability scanning and test generation. Broader scope than RAGAS but less focused on RAG-specific metrics.

name: TruLens
summary: LLM app evaluation with feedback functions for groundedness, relevance, and sentiment. Snowflake-acquired. Combines evaluation with tracing/observability. Now part of Snowflake Cortex ecosystem.

name: LangSmith
summary: LangChain's observability and evaluation platform. Tracing, dataset management, online evaluation, and prompt playground. Best-in-class LangChain integration but proprietary SaaS with ecosystem lock-in.

name: Braintrust
summary: LLM evaluation and observability platform with proxy-based logging. Dataset management, experiment tracking, and online scoring. Provider-agnostic alternative to LangSmith.

name: Humanloop
summary: Prompt management and evaluation platform. Version-controlled prompts with A/B testing and human feedback collection. Enterprise-focused; strong for teams iterating on prompts collaboratively.

name: PEFT
summary: HuggingFace's parameter-efficient fine-tuning library (LoRA, QLoRA, IA3, etc.). Standard for fine-tuning LLMs on consumer/HPC GPUs. Dramatically reduces memory requirements. Essential for HPC fine-tuning.

name: Axolotl
summary: YAML-config LLM fine-tuning framework. Wraps HF Transformers + PEFT + DeepSpeed into a single config file. Supports LoRA, QLoRA, full fine-tune, DPO, RLHF. Community favorite for fine-tuning.

name: LLaMA Factory
summary: All-in-one LLM fine-tuning framework with web UI. Supports 100+ models, multiple training methods (SFT, RLHF, DPO, KTO). Most starred fine-tuning repo. Web UI lowers barrier for beginners.

name: Unsloth
summary: 2-5x faster LLM fine-tuning with 70% less memory via custom Triton kernels. Drop-in replacement for HF Trainer. Ideal for memory-constrained HPC. Free tier with Pro for multi-GPU.

name: TRL
summary: HuggingFace's Transformer Reinforcement Learning library. SFT, RLHF (PPO), DPO, KTO trainers built on HF Transformers. Official path for alignment training. Integrates with PEFT for efficient training.

name: OpenAI Fine-tuning API
summary: OpenAI's managed fine-tuning service. Upload JSONL, get a fine-tuned model. Zero infrastructure but expensive, limited customization, and full vendor lock-in. No weight access.

name: Ollama Library
summary: Ollama's curated model registry with one-command pull. Docker Hub-like experience for LLMs. Limited to GGUF format models. Simple but less comprehensive than HuggingFace Hub.

name: OpenRouter
summary: Unified API gateway for 100+ LLM providers. OpenAI-compatible API with automatic fallback routing. Pay-per-token across providers. Great for model comparison; adds latency and a dependency.

name: Together AI
summary: Inference and fine-tuning platform for open-source models. Competitive pricing, OpenAI-compatible API, and serverless endpoints. Strong for open-weight model hosting without managing infrastructure.

name: Replicate
summary: Cloud platform for running ML models via API. Cog container format for packaging. Pay-per-second GPU pricing. Broad model ecosystem beyond LLMs (image, audio, video).

name: Guardrails AI
summary: Input/output validation framework for LLMs. Validator Hub with pre-built checks for PII, toxicity, hallucination, etc. RAIL spec for declarative validation. Growing ecosystem of community validators.

name: NeMo Guardrails
summary: NVIDIA's programmable guardrails with Colang DSL. Topic control, jailbreak prevention, and fact-checking rails. More structured than Guardrails AI but steeper learning curve with custom DSL.

name: LLM Guard
summary: Input/output scanner for LLM interactions. Detects prompt injection, PII, toxicity, and code injection. Local model-based scanning (no API calls needed). Good for air-gapped/HPC environments.

name: Lakera
summary: AI security platform with Lakera Guard API. Prompt injection detection, content moderation, and PII detection as a service. Low-latency API; enterprise-focused with SOC2 compliance.

name: Rebuff
summary: Prompt injection detection using multi-layer approach (heuristics, LLM-based, vector similarity). Early-stage; limited maintenance. Proof-of-concept quality; consider LLM Guard for production.

name: Pydantic AI
summary: From Pydantic team. Agent framework with type-safe structured output, dependency injection, and Logfire integration. Early but high-quality code. Natural fit for Pydantic-heavy codebases.

name: BAML
summary: Domain-specific language for LLM functions with type-safe structured output. Compiler generates client code from .baml files. Novel approach with VS Code playground. Growing but requires buying into DSL.

name: Inngest
summary: Event-driven workflow engine with built-in AI agent support. Step functions with retries, concurrency, and throttling. Growing AI/LLM orchestration focus. TypeScript-first.

name: Trigger.dev
summary: Background job framework for TypeScript with long-running task support. v3 added AI/LLM task patterns. Self-hostable alternative to cloud functions for AI workloads. TypeScript-only ecosystem.

name: Langfuse
summary: Open-source LLM observability with tracing, prompt management, and evaluation. Self-hostable LangSmith alternative. Growing fast as the open-source default for LLM monitoring. Docker-based self-hosting.

name: Helicone
summary: LLM proxy for logging, monitoring, and cost tracking. One-line integration via URL swap. Request caching, rate limiting, and usage dashboards. Lightest-touch observability option.

name: Portkey
summary: AI gateway with observability, caching, load balancing, and fallback routing. Unified API for 200+ LLMs. Production-focused with guardrails integration. Combines gateway + observability in one tool.

name: Opik
summary: Comet ML's open-source LLM evaluation and tracing platform. Combines experiment tracking heritage with LLM-specific features. Self-hostable. Newer entrant competing with Langfuse.

name: GitHub Copilot
summary: GitHub's AI coding assistant. Inline completions, chat, and workspace agent. Market leader with deepest IDE integration. Subscription-based; no offline mode. Agent mode growing rapidly.

name: Cursor
summary: AI-native code editor (VS Code fork). Codebase-aware chat, multi-file editing, and composer mode. Fastest-growing AI code editor. Subscription model; VS Code fork means some extension compatibility.

name: Codeium
summary: Free AI code completion with broad IDE support. Parent company of Windsurf editor. Generous free tier. Less capable than Copilot/Cursor but zero cost for individuals.

name: Continue
summary: Open-source AI code assistant for VS Code and JetBrains. BYO model (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic). Fully local-capable with Ollama. Best open-source Copilot alternative.

name: Aider
summary: Terminal-based AI pair programming tool. Git-aware with automatic commits. Works with any LLM provider. Best CLI-based coding assistant; ideal for SSH/HPC environments without GUI.

name: Claude Code
summary: Anthropic's agentic CLI coding tool. Full shell access, file editing, git integration, and MCP tool use. Terminal-native; excellent for HPC/SSH. Claude-only but most capable agentic coding tool.

name: Windsurf
summary: AI code editor from Codeium (VS Code fork). Cascade flow-based multi-file editing. Competes directly with Cursor. Generous free tier; acquired by OpenAI in 2025.

name: Tabnine
summary: AI code completion with on-premise deployment option. Enterprise-focused with privacy guarantees. Pioneer in AI code completion but losing ground to Copilot and Cursor. Self-hosted option for compliance.

name: Amazon Q Developer
summary: AWS's AI coding assistant (formerly CodeWhisperer). Strong AWS service integration and security scanning. Free tier available. Best for AWS-heavy teams; less capable than Copilot/Cursor for general coding.

name: litellm
summary: Unified API for 100+ LLM providers. OpenAI-compatible interface with fallbacks, load balancing, and spend tracking. Proxy server mode for team usage. De facto standard for multi-provider LLM access.

name: Docling
summary: IBM's document parsing library. PDF, DOCX, HTML to structured output with layout-aware chunking. ML-based table and figure extraction. Strong alternative to Unstructured for academic/research docs.
